Title: Altered circadian rhythms regulate growth vigor in hybrids and allopolyploids

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mentioned as a possible additional factor mediating TOC1 and GI expression, but no direct nutrient-metabolism validation is presented in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ELF4 is mentioned only as an alternative factor that may mediate TOC1 and GI expression. 2. No direct experimental evidence connects ELF4 to chlorophyll or starch changes in this study. 3. It should be treated as contextual rather than a core experimentally validated metabolism gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in the user-provided key gene list but not a core gene based on results-focused evidence in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in a light-dependent step during chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of PORA is associated with increased chlorophyll accumulation in allotetraploids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PORA contains EE/CBS-related promoter context and is upregulated in allotetraploids when CCA1/LHY are repressed. 2. CCA1 binds the PORA promoter fragment in EMSA, supporting direct clock regulation. 3. Increased PORA expression correlates with elevated chlorophyll content and growth vigor.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in seedlings and young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod; sampling around ZT6 (noon)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increasing PORA expression may be useful to enhance chlorophyll accumulation and vigor, potentially in combination with circadian regulators
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in allotetraploids; EMSA for CCA1 binding; metabolite profiling of chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior evidence that PORA overexpression increases chlorophyll content, viability, and growth vigor.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in a light-dependent step during chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of PORB is associated with increased chlorophyll accumulation in allotetraploids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PORB is upregulated in allotetraploids during daytime repression of CCA1/LHY. 2. Recombinant CCA1 specifically binds the PORB promoter fragment by EMSA. 3. Elevated PORB expression correlates with higher chlorophyll content in hybrids/allotetraploids.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana × Arabidopsis arenosa allotetraploids / Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in seedlings and young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod; sampling around ZT6 (noon)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for engineering higher chlorophyll content, especially alongside circadian tuning
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in allotetraploids; EMSA for CCA1 binding; chlorophyll measurements
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior evidence that overexpressing PORA/PORB increases chlorophyll content and growth vigor.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucosyl units among maltooligosaccharides during starch breakdown/remodeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for starch metabolism and sugar release
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbohydrate storage supporting growth and biomass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DPE1 is upregulated in allotetraploids and repressed by ectopic CCA1 expression, linking circadian regulation to elevated starch metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DPE1 is among EE/CBS-containing starch metabolic genes upregulated in allotetraploids. 2. CCA1 binds the DPE1 promoter fragment in EMSA. 3. DPE1 expression decreases in TOC1:CCA1 plants and increases when CCA1 is reduced, paralleling starch changes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at ZT6 in allotetraploids; repressed in TOC1:CCA1 plants; induced in cca1-related repression backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for circadian-guided engineering of carbohydrate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; EMSA; transgenic TOC1:CCA1 and TOC1:cca1-RNAi comparisons; starch assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats DPE1 as a downstream CCA1 target in starch metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates glucans involved in starch turnover/remodeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for starch metabolism/remodeling affecting starch accumulation dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbohydrate storage supporting growth and biomass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GWD3 is a CCA1-responsive starch metabolism gene whose increased expression correlates with enhanced starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWD3 is upregulated in allotetraploids when CCA1/LHY are repressed. 2. CCA1 specifically binds the GWD3 promoter fragment in EMSA. 3. GWD/GWD3 expression is repressed in TOC1:CCA1 lines and induced when CCA1 is reduced, accompanying altered starch content.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at ZT6 in allotetraploids; reduced in TOC1:CCA1; increased in CCA1-repressed backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for increasing leaf starch through circadian/metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; EMSA; transgenic perturbation of CCA1; starch quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to GWD3 as one of the direct CCA1-bound downstream genes involved in starch metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,4-glucosidic linkages in starch during starch breakdown
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Participates in starch turnover linked to carbohydrate accumulation dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transient carbohydrate storage supporting growth and biomass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AMY3 expression is inversely related to CCA1 activity and is associated with altered starch metabolism in transgenic and mutant plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMY3 is among starch metabolic genes upregulated in allotetraploids. 2. AMY expression is strongly repressed in TOC1:CCA1 plants and induced in cca1/cca1 lhy/TOC1:cca1-RNAi backgrounds. 3. Expression changes parallel altered leaf starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in allotetraploids; repressed in TOC1:CCA1; induced in CCA1-repressed backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream metabolic node for tuning leaf carbohydrate metabolism with circadian regulators
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in allotetraploids, mutants, and transgenics; starch assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct CCA1 binding to AMY3 was not explicitly shown in the main text, unlike PORA/PORB/DPE1/GWD3.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Single MYB-domain circadian clock transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of downstream EE/CBS-containing genes and reciprocal clock targets
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chlorophyll biosynthesis and starch metabolism via circadian output regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TOC1; PORA; PORB; DPE1; GWD3; AMY
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TOC1 and multiple EE/CBS-containing downstream metabolic genes during the day; reduction of CCA1 relieves repression and increases expression of chlorophyll/starch pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major daytime repressor whose reduced expression in hybrids/allotetraploids is causally linked to increased chlorophyll and starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthesis, carbon fixation support, transient carbohydrate storage, biomass accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Daytime repression or mutation/RNAi reduction of CCA1 increases downstream metabolic gene expression, chlorophyll and starch accumulation, and contributes to growth vigor; constitutive or ectopic CCA1 expression has the opposite effect
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCA1 is epigenetically repressed in allotetraploids/hybrids during the day, especially the A. thaliana locus. 2. Reduced CCA1 is associated with increased TOC1, GI, PORA, PORB and starch-metabolism genes and with elevated chlorophyll, starch and sugar levels. 3. Causality is supported by EMSA/ChIP, TOC1:CCA1 overexpression, cca1 mutant analysis and TOC1:cca1-RNAi plants.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Allo733; Allo738; Columbia; C24 hybrids; cca1-11; cca1-11 lhy-21
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TOC1 promoter (inducible/clock-regulated); 35S constitutive promoter in CCA1-OX background referenced
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Circadian/autonomous clock regulation; chromatin state; light-dark cycle
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Reciprocal feedback loop with TOC1 and GI; partially redundant with LHY
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks at dawn, decreases by noon, lowest toward dusk, rises again later
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Repressed 2–4 fold at ZT6–12 in allotetraploids and F1 hybrids; higher than MPV at ZT15
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 22°C; 16-h light/8-h dark; daytime versus dusk sampling (ZT0, ZT6, ZT15)
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter-associated H3K9Ac and H3K4Me2 are reduced 2–3 fold in allotetraploids, consistent with repression; A. thaliana locus is more strongly repressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/CAPS locus-specific expression; ChIP for histone marks and CCA1 binding; EMSA; TOC1:CCA1 transgenics; CCA1-OX; cca1 and cca1 lhy mutants; TOC1:cca1-RNAi; chlorophyll/starch/sugar assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial daytime reduction of CCA1 may be a strategy to enhance biomass and carbohydrate accumulation, but severe disruption of clock function may impose penalties
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive CCA1 causes reduced chlorophyll/starch and late flowering; severe clock disruption in cca1 lhy lowers starch relative to cca1 single mutant; flowering-time alterations occur in transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper presents CCA1 as the key causal regulator linking circadian epigenetic modulation to metabolic vigor.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Single MYB-domain circadian clock transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression within the central clock and likely of EE/CBS-containing outputs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Circadian regulation of chlorophyll and starch metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TOC1; GI; EE/CBS-containing downstream genes (inferred from functional overlap with CCA1)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts partially redundantly with CCA1 to repress TOC1/GI and downstream clock output genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important co-regulator, but the paper provides stronger direct causal evidence for CCA1 than for LHY
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthesis support and carbohydrate storage contributing to vigor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Daytime repression of LHY accompanies increased downstream metabolic gene expression and elevated chlorophyll/starch accumulation in allotetraploids and hybrids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHY is repressed in allotetraploids and F1 hybrids during the day, inversely correlating with TOC1/GI expression. 2. Its promoters show reduced activating histone marks in allotetraploids. 3. cca1 lhy double mutant analysis indicates that excessive loss of clock regulation can reduce the beneficial metabolic effect seen in cca1 single mutant.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Allo733; Allo738; C24 × Columbia hybrids; cca1-11 lhy-21
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Circadian/autonomous clock regulation; chromatin state; light-dark cycle
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Reciprocal feedback with TOC1/GI; partial redundancy with CCA1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks at dawn and declines toward dusk
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Repressed at ZT6–12 in allotetraploids and hybrids; elevated near dusk
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter H3K9Ac and H3K4Me2 are reduced in allotetraploids, consistent with repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/CAPS; ChIP for histone marks; mutant analysis with cca1 lhy double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning LHY together with CCA1 may modulate biomass-related metabolism, but strong loss of oscillator function may be detrimental
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double loss with cca1 reduces starch relative to cca1 single mutant, indicating clock-disruption penalty
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding of LHY to metabolic targets was not shown in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive reciprocal regulation of CCA1/LHY within central clock feedback; promoter used for clock-timed transgene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Circadian timing of downstream metabolic outputs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CCA1; LHY (reciprocal positive regulation in the paper's model)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively regulates CCA1/LHY in reciprocal feedback and is itself repressed by CCA1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Secondary central clock node associated with elevated metabolic output when upregulated at noon, but not the main causal gene tested for nutrient metabolite control
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports photosynthetic and carbohydrate rhythmic regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated at noon in allotetraploids/hybrids inversely with CCA1/LHY, accompanying increased chlorophyll and starch pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOC1 expression increases when CCA1/LHY are repressed in allotetraploids and hybrids. 2. Its promoter shows increased H3K9Ac and H3K4Me2 in allotetraploids. 3. CCA1 directly binds the TOC1 promoter by EMSA/ChIP, placing TOC1 in the causal feedback loop linked to metabolic vigor.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Allo733; Allo738; C24 × Columbia hybrids; Columbia transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TOC1 promoter (clock-regulated) used to drive CCA1 and cca1-RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Central circadian feedback; chromatin modifications; light-dark cycle
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher at noon than dawn, inverse to CCA1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at ZT6 in allotetraploids and F1 hybrids
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter H3K9Ac and H3K4Me2 are increased in allotetraploids, consistent with upregulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a clock-regulated promoter source for timed gene expression rather than as a direct metabolic engineering target in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic TOC1:CCA1 expression reduced chlorophyll/starch and altered flowering via clock perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although listed among key genes, TOC1 primarily serves as a central clock component and transgene promoter in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive reciprocal regulation with CCA1/LHY within clock network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with CCA1/LHY feedback and with clock-mediated metabolic outputs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Associated regulator rather than the principal causal target in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports rhythmic metabolism and biomass accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GI is upregulated in allotetraploids when CCA1/LHY are repressed, correlating with increased starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GI expression is inversely correlated with CCA1/LHY in allotetraploids. 2. GI promoters show increased activating histone marks in allotetraploids. 3. The paper notes GI induction correlates with starch accumulation, although prior literature reports gi mutants can also increase starch, indicating complexity.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Circadian/autonomous regulation; chromatin state; light-dark cycle
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inverse to CCA1/LHY, higher at noon
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter H3K9Ac and H3K4Me2 are increased in allotetraploids
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential clock-network component for tuning vigor, but application is uncertain because of context-dependent starch and flowering effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: GI-related manipulations may alter flowering time and have complex starch phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The authors explicitly note the complexity that gi mutants can increase starch, whereas GI induction here correlates with starch accumulation.
